steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence: "However, many dinosaurs that didn't have wings also had feathers on their bodies." The word However signals a contrast with the opening claim that feathered dinosaurs are typically thought of as winged ones. The sentence must be doing something to correct or expand that narrow view.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It reveals that the dinosaurs mentioned earlier in the text used their feathers for an important purpose.': The sentence says nothing about what feathers were used for — no purpose is mentioned. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It gives an example of a dinosaur with the characteristics mentioned earlier in the text.': The example is Ornithomimus, which appears in the next sentence. The underlined sentence makes the general claim; it is not itself an example. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It defines a term related to the type of dinosaur feathers mentioned earlier in the text.': No definition is offered anywhere in the underlined sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It indicates that the dinosaurs mentioned earlier in the text weren't the only ones that had feathers.': Exactly right. Winged dinosaurs like Microraptor were mentioned; the underlined sentence uses "However" to show that feathers weren't exclusive to them — wingless dinosaurs had them too. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
